channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Ruiz Re entra en País Vasco con la integración de Abando 2000
item.description: Ruiz Re ha iniciado su expansión en País Vasco con la integración de la correduría bilbaína Abando 2000, especializada tanto en seguros como en asesoramiento patrimonial y financiero. Según explica Juan David Ruiz, el grupo prevé anunciar nuevas incorporaciones en País Vasco en las próximas semanas y celebrará en Bilbao su convención nacional de 2027 como muestra de su apuesta por el norte. Además, Abando 2000 cuenta con una actividad destacada en el ámbito financiero, con 5 millones de euros en fondos de inversión y 5,5 millones en planes de pensiones, lo que eleva el patrimonio total gestionado hasta los 10,5 millones de euros .

item.title: Los planes de pensiones individuales alcanzan el 11,3% de rentabilidad anual en abril
item.description: La rentabilidad interanual para el conjunto de planes de pensiones del Sistema Individual se situó en abril en el 11,3% , según datos facilitados por Inverco, que destaca el comportamiento de los productos de Renta Variable, que alcanzan una revalorización del 26,3% a 1 año, 15,7% a 3 años y a 5 años un 10,1% . A más largo plazo ( 20 años ), los planes registraron una rentabilidad media anual neta del 3,1% . Netas de gastos y comisiones En abril, el volumen de activos del Sistema Individual se situó en 98.283 millones de euros, tras experimentar un incremento de 3.543 millones de euros debido a las revalorizaciones de los mercados financieros.

item.title: Sigue el movimiento de ahorradores hacia productos con más potencial de rentabilidad
item.description: El mayor dinamismo en captación de nuevos asegurados corresponde a los productos con mayor exposición a los mercados financieros. En el extremo opuesto, los productos que pierden cartera son precisamente aquellos cuyo ahorro gestionado también retrocede. El sector asegurador cerró el primer trimestre con 31.277.368 asegurados en seguros de vida —un 3,06% más que un año antes— y 5.524.648 partícipes en planes de pensiones gestionados por aseguradoras , que crecieron a un ritmo aún mayor, del 7,38% interanual.

item.title: Snap, YouTube Settle School-Social Media Suit Ahead of Trial
item.description: TikTok and Snap previously settled a first-of-its-kind personal injury suit over social media addiction shortly before it went to trial in Los Angeles earlier this year. reached agreements to settle the first lawsuit headed to trial over claims that addiction to top social media platforms has disrupted learning and pushed public schools to spend massive sums fighting a mental health crisis, according to court filings. Related: Meta Asks California Judge to Throw out Landmark Social Media Addiction Verdict The trial will serve as a test case for more than 1,200 similar lawsuits nationwide in which school districts allege that the biggest social media companies have harmed students so badly that it’s undermining the education system.

item.title: US Sees A 27% Increase in Recalled Products in Q1, Report Shows
item.description: recall report shows the volume of defective products recalled across five U.S. According to the report, the consumer products sector was the only industry to record an increase in recalls events, rising 14.5%. The automotive sector had its highest quarterly total in two years with 12.19 million units recalled, while the pharmaceutical sector reached a four-year high with 218.83 million units impacted, the report shows.

item.title: Steadfast-owned Miramar acquires Australian sports specialist MGA
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Kiwi broker Rothbury appoints Aon veteran Charlene Kitching as head of broking services
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Guy Carpenter names Andrew Hare as head of growth for APAC
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA and Bank of England set out shared vision for tokenisation in UK wholesale markets
item.description: UK financial firms can adopt tokenisation and distributed ledger technology (DLT) with greater confidence, as the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and the Bank of England set out a shared vision and seek industry views on the future of UK wholesale markets. UK markets have always embraced new technology, and that will be central to ensuring the UK remains at the forefront of global wholesale markets.' Sarah Breeden, deputy governor for financial stability at the Bank of England said: 'The Bank and FCA have done a huge amount to enable the responsible adoption of tokenisation in retail and wholesale finance in the UK, working with the government and the industry. We want to support firms in adopting this technology to lower costs, reduce risk and unlock new services, and our partnership with the Bank of England will ensure a common approach across all parts of wholesale markets.

item.title: TWIA secures $750m of reinsurance from Alamo Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: Before going into the final details on these Alamo Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond notes, here’s what we now know about TWIA’s reinsurance tower for 2026 so far. (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond sponsorship, securing the upper-end target for $750 million of capital markets backed reinsurance limit from this issuance, Artemis can report. The Texas Windstorm Insurance Association (TWIA), the residual market property insurer for the State of Texas, returned to the cat bond market in April with an initial target to secure $450 million of limit from this Alamo Re 2026-1 issuance .

item.title: Nationwide Mutual secures $350m of reinsurance from Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: Nationwide Mutual ventured back to the catastrophe bond market in April, with an initial target to secure $200 million of multi-peril and multi-year indemnity triggered and per-occurrence based reinsurance protection from this Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond transaction. Now, we’re told that Nationwide Mutual has priced the notes to successfully secure the upsized $350 million of reinsurance from this new Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond, while securing the coverage at the low-ends of reduced price guidance. With the two tranches of Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 cat bond notes now priced, we know that this new cat bond sponsorship with provide Nationwide Mutual with $350 million of protection against losses from the perils of US named storm, earthquake, severe thunderstorm, winter storm, wildfire, meteorite impact, volcanic eruption.

item.title: Jamaica now targets upsized $200m of parametric insurance from new catastrophe bond
item.description: The Government of Jamaica has raised the target size for its third catastrophe bond, now aiming to secure $200 million of parametric named storm and hurricane insurance protection from the World Bank facilitated IBRD CAR Jamaica 2026 issuance, market sources have told Artemis. Jamaica returned for its third catastrophe bond sponsorship at the end of April, initially targeting $150 million of parametric named storm and hurricane protection . Jamaica first secured parametric hurricane insurance disaster risk protection from the capital markets through the $185 million IBRD CAR 130 cat bond in 2021.

item.title: Cyber attacks drive £3.7bn in shareholder litigation costs for UK businesses, Gallagher research finds
item.description: Cyber attacks generated an estimated £11.7 billion in losses for large UK businesses during 2025, with shareholder litigation making up £3.7 billion of the total cost, according to new research from Gallagher, the international insurance brokerage, risk management and consulting firm, in partnership with the Centre for Economics and Business Research (CEBR). Gallagher warned that if the financial impact of cyber-attacks rises by a further 5% in 2026, including disruption, legal claims and recovery expenses, annual losses for large UK businesses could exceed £12 billion. Gallagher and CEBR found that shareholder litigation represented the second largest area of financial loss, behind £5.4 billion linked to business interruption and disrupted trading activity.

item.title: Generali and ECMWF formalise European partnership on climate risk and resilience
item.description: The European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF), the European intergovernmental organisation specialising in weather and climate forecasting, has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with international insurer and asset manager Generali to expand the use of quality-assured climate information in support of climate risk resilience across Europe. Through the collaboration, Generali’s Group Climate Hub will work alongside the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) and the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS), both delivered by ECMWF on behalf of the European Commission. ECMWF and Generali said the partnership reflects growing demand for reliable climate and atmospheric data as extreme weather events continue to increase in both frequency and intensity.

item.title: OdysseyRe appoints Gromellon and Serres as Co-CUOs, EMEA
item.description: Odyssey Reinsurance Company (OdysseyRe), the property and casualty reinsurance arm of Odyssey Group Holdings, Inc., a subsidiary of Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited, has appointed Tegwen Gromellon and Anne-Claire Serres as Co-Chief Underwriting Officers (CUO) of its Europe, Middle East and Asia (EMEA) division, effective July 1st, 2026. Serres joined OdysseyRe in 2018 as the Market Director for France and is based in Paris. Gromellon joined OdysseyRe in 2004 and has served as Market Director for Germany, Austria, Benelux, and Switzerland since 2019.

item.title: Consumers Sue Amazon for Not Refunding Trump Tariff Costs
item.description: Related: Nike Sued by Consumers for Not Refunding Tariff Costs Amazon.com Inc was sued on Friday by consumers seeking refunds for costs passed on to them in the form of higher prices as a result of tariffs the U.S. Consumers in a proposed class action filed in federal court in Seattle alleged that the e-commerce giant collected hundreds of millions of dollars in unlawful tariff costs by raising prices on imported goods before the Supreme Court had ruled.

item.title: Disaster Resilience Investment Has Touched $100 Billion in Asia
item.description: Governments, investors and banks have poured about $100 billion into efforts to handle the effects of climate change in Asia in the past five years, with water and infrastructure projects attracting the majority of the capital. More than 90% of the investment between 2021 and 2025 was made by state-related organizations or development finance institutions, according to a study of 165 funding groups published Monday. The study mainly analyzed investments in China, India and Southeast Asia.

item.title: UK Firms Should Take Steps to Limit Risks From Frontier AI Models, UK Says
item.description: British companies should take steps to plan for and mitigate risks from new artificial intelligence models, the country’s finance ministry, the Bank of England and the Financial Conduct Authority regulator said on Friday. “The cyber capabilities of current frontier AI models are already exceeding what a skilled practitioner could achieve, and at a significantly higher speed, greater scale, and lower cost,” they said in a joint statement. “These capabilities, if used maliciously, amplify cyber threats to firms’ safety and soundness, customers, market integrity, and financial stability.” Last month BoE governor Andrew Bailey said he saw major cybersecurity risks from Anthropic’s Mythos product.

item.title: Asia Pacific MGA market has ‘a lot of headroom’ for growth
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Maine Mourns 27-Year-Old Firefighter Killed in Lumber Mill Explosion
item.description: A 27-year-old firefighter killed in a huge fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine’s midcoast region was honored Saturday, a day after the incident that injured at least 11 others. Officials on Saturday identified Cross as the firefighter who was killed. The procession routed through the small town of fewer than 1,000 people on its way from Augusta to Belfast, Maine, a journey about 46 miles (74 kilometers) long.

item.title: Chubb partners with Verified Clinical Trials
item.description: Verified Clinical Trials (VCT), a provider of clinical trial subject registry solutions, announced a strategic alliance with Chubb focused on reducing risks tied to duplicate enrollment and protocol violations in clinical trials. Mitchell Efros, Co-Founder & CEO at Verified Clinical Trials. Under the alliance, Chubb clients will gain access to VCT’s registry capabilities as part of a broader clinical trial risk management approach.

item.title: RT Specialty promotes Will Jacob to president of RT Connector
item.description: RT Specialty announced that it has promoted Will Jacob to President, RT Connector, effective immediately. RT Connector remains a differentiated growth engine for our wholesale brokerage operation and solves our clients’ need to transact business quickly and efficiently using technology, all while backed by RT’s industry-leading underwriting talent.” – Michael VanAcker, Co-President of RT Specialty. Will has been with Ryan Specialty for nearly 10 years and was most recently Chief Underwriting Officer for RT Connector, joining the team during the infancy of the electronic platform.

item.title: Santalucía discloses cybersecurity incident involving customer data
item.description: Spanish insurer Santalucía disclosed a cybersecurity incident involving unauthorized access to customer information and said it activated its contingency plan in response. Santalucía added that it reinforced system monitoring and deployed additional resources to protect customer data. The incident has been contained and did not result in service interruptions, data modification, or operational disruption.

item.title: Trucordia acquires Idaho agency Richardson Insurance
item.description: Trucordia announced the acquisition of the assets of Idaho based Richardson Insurance, a personal and commercial lines agency founded in 1988. Richardson Insurance was established by Randy Richardson and expanded from auto insurance into mortgage and real estate related coverage over the years. The deal expands Trucordia’s presence in Idaho and adds a business with longstanding community relationships and a strong client retention record.

item.title: Trump’s US$40bn Hormuz insurance scheme non-starter due to lack of US naval escorts: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Farmers names Dentsu creative agency of record
item.description: Farmers appointed Dentsu Creative as its creative agency of record, tasking the agency with leading brand, performance, social, and sponsorship marketing efforts as the insurer looks to strengthen brand positioning and customer engagement. As we continue to evolve, we are excited to work with Dentsu Creative to help shape the future of our brand and support the growth of our business.” – Eleanor Solomon, Head of Creative for Farmers Insurance. Our goal is to create work that is unmistakably Farmers, designed to thrive and flex in today’s ever-changing environment.” – Julie Scelzo, Chief Creative Officer, U.S., Dentsu Creative.

item.title: Willis launches carbon capture insurance solution
item.description: Willis, a business of WTW , launched a Carbon Capture and Storage ( CCS ) insurance solution designed to support developers and operators across the CO₂ capture, transportation, and storage value chain. “We are delighted to announce the launch of our CCS solution, designed to support the next phase of carbon capture and storage deployment. The integrated offering combines energy, marine, liability, and environmental insurance protections tailored to CCS projects and includes support for FEED phase risk engineering, contractual risk allocation, lender and regulatory requirements, and carbon credit obligations.

item.title: Pharmacists Mutual collaborates with Positive Physicians
item.description: Pharmacists Mutual Insurance Company announced a strategic collaboration with Positive Physicians as part of its long term growth strategy in the healthcare professional liability market. The collaboration is intended to expand Pharmacists Mutual’s professional liability capabilities through participation in select physician liability segments via Positive Physicians’ partner driven “Positive Select Program,” which focuses on underwriting discipline and targeted risk selection. “We are excited to collaborate with Positive Physicians on this initiative,” said Aaron Pearce, president and CEO of Pharmacists Mutual Insurance Group.

item.title: Trucordia Launches AI-Enabled Personal Lines Tool
item.description: Trucordia (Lindon, Utah) has launched Trucordia Go, an AI-enabled tool designed to help consumers explore auto and home insurance options. The result is fast and conversational, with optional support from licensed insurance professionals.” Trucordia says the tool can help consumers understand coverage options, identify coverage gaps and potential savings, and evaluate when a change may be needed. Trucordia Go will help empower a more informed and confident client to make the best insurance decisions for their personal needs.” Trucordia says the launch reflects its continued investment in technology to support the client experience and create a digital entry point to its licensed insurance professionals.

item.title: LexisNexis Launches Home Risk Location Model
item.description: LexisNexis Risk Solutions (Atlanta) has launched LexisNexis Location Intelligence for Home, an AI-driven predictive model designed to help U.S. The company says the capability combines industry-wide home claims data, location-based insights and predictive modeling to generate property-level risk scores. Location Intelligence for Home is available through LexisNexis Smart Selection, the company’s automated data service for inspection flags and customizable business rules.

item.title: Softening market tests underwriting discipline
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anduril Leads Varied Lineup Of Large Deals
item.description: Defense tech unicorn Anduril Industries led the fundraising lineup in a week heavy with rounds for companies focused on applications in the physical world. This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here .

item.title: Lemon apunta a operar sin terceros en Perú para mejorar costos y velocidad
item.description: La estrategia de construir con socios y luego aplicar a una licencia propia para acelerar productos y mejorar eficiencia operativa tiene un nuevo capítulo en Perú, donde la billetera fiat-cripto Lemon acaba de solicitar un permiso que perfila hacia dónde podrían moverse las billeteras digitales. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Scotiabank Chile prepara BNPL y Bunq entra a México
item.description: Scotiabank Chile alista el lanzamiento de Buy Now, Pay Later , según su gerente país, Diego Masola. El neobanco holandés Bunq solicitó autorización para operar como banco en México , en una señal de la creciente competencia digital en el sistema financiero local. La firma apunta a atender a “ciudadanos globales” y suma a México a su estrategia de expansión, que también incluye Estados Unidos.

item.title: Life Insurers Face a New Longevity Reality
item.description: We’re facing a new longevity reality in America: people are living longer than ever before — a testament to many incredible advances in science, technology and healthcare — but at the same time, the gap between healthspan and lifespan is growing. John Hancock ’s (Boston) Longevity Preparedness Index (LPI) —created in collaboration with the MIT AgeLab—offers a first-of-its-kind benchmark for how ready American adults are to live well in older age across eight key areas: social connection, finance, daily activities, care, home, community, health, and life transitions. As a result, we face an urgent question: are we truly prepared to live longer?

item.title: Ivans, Cytora Target Commercial Submission Friction
item.description: For decades, Ivans (Tampa, Fla.) has occupied a largely invisible but essential place in the insurance industry’s infrastructure. Applied Systems (Chicago) acquired the property/casualty business of Ivans in 2013. “Others have used the phrase ‘plumbing’ or ‘connectivity layer’ or ‘infrastructure utility,’” comments Michael Streit, President, Ivans.

item.title: Applied’s Graham Blackwell Sees AI Accelerating Connected Insurance
item.description: Source: Applied Systems.) Applied Systems (Chicago) CEO-designate Graham Blackwell says the company’s next phase will focus on accelerating what it calls the “Digital Roundtrip of Insurance,” using AI and workflow automation to connect agencies, carriers and wholesalers more tightly across the insurance lifecycle. (Graham Blackwell, who will assume the CEO role for Applied Systems later this year. Graham Blackwell, President and CEO-designate, Applied Systems.

item.title: Judge Considers Anthropic’s $1.5 Billion Settlement of Authors’ Lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday pressed lawyers for more information on several points of artificial intelligence company Anthropic’s proposed $1.5 billion settlement with authors who accused it of misusing their books to train its AI chatbot Claude. Related: OpenAI Floats Idea of Global AI Governance Body With US, China Authors and other copyright holders filed claims covering over 92% of the more than 480,000 works included in the settlement, an attorney for the authors said during the hearing. The settlement has spurred objections from authors who have argued it is not large enough, overcompensates the plaintiffs’ attorneys or wrongly excludes some copyright owners.

item.title: FCA, Bank of England and Treasury joint statement on frontier AI models and cyber resilience
item.description: Frontier AI models represent a step-change in capability, with significant implications for cyber security and operational resilience. The cyber capabilities of current frontier AI models are already exceeding what a skilled practitioner could achieve, and at a significantly higher speed, greater scale, and lower cost. Firms should read and consider the effective practices on cyber resilience published by the Bank, PRA and FCA in October 2025.

item.title: Integral ILS encouraged by AuM growth in larger, more sophisticated market: Lowther and Sannemalm
item.description: Artemis recently met with Richard Lowther, Co-Founder and Managing Partner and Kristofer Sannemalm, Head of Underwriting for Integral ILS, at their office in Bermuda, to discuss market trends and Integral’s approach as the manager’s assets under management sits at a healthy $3.5 billion as of January 2026. Today, Lowther feels that the asset class has evolved with more sophistication at the broker level in matching risk to various ILS market appetites, as well as consultants having a better understanding of the unique attributes of ILS platforms to guide their client’s investment strategies. Expanding on this, Sannemalm emphasised that the structural maturity of the asset through its strong track record and industry adopted catastrophe risk models means that today, “the due diligence process is more streamlined and comprehensive to better highlight the bespoke advantages each manager brings to the table, and whether they align with a client’s investment philosophy.” Over the years, transparency has improved in the ILS space, but while it’s important to work with investors to give them what they want, Lowther stressed that tailoring based on individual client needs is critical as one size of reporting does not necessarily fit all.

item.title: Pro Global strengthens LatAm offering to support growth and a complex insurance market
item.description: Pro Global, a specialist adviser to the specialist insurance sector, has strategically strengthened its offering in Latin America to support growth and increasing operational complexity in the insurance market. “These services, which Pro Global already delivers in the Latin American market, form part of an integrated proposition that complements its established capabilities in claims management, consulting, digital transformation, underwriting support, and operational services. Together, they position Pro Global as a strategic partner capable of supporting clients across the entire insurance value chain, in both local operations and increasingly complex international environments,” Pro Global stated.

item.title: Evolving geopolitical landscape is creating new opportunities in the Middle East: Pelagos CEO
item.description: The evolving geopolitical landscape in the Middle East is opening new opportunities in the region, Dan Burrows, Pelagos Insurance Capital CEO, commented during the company’s recent earnings call. The evolving geopolitical landscape is creating new opportunities in this region, which we are well positioned to continue executing on.” The executive pointed to the company’s rapid operational response as a key differentiator following the initial escalation of tensions in the region. Demonstrating our ability to not only match the right capital to the right risk, but also to the right partner at the right time.” Burrows also commented on how the geopolitical events in the Middle East impacted this quarter, highlighting that the financial tailwinds from these accounts will manifest more later in the year.

item.title: Centre for Disaster Protection’s new platform to help accelerate risk financing solutions
item.description: The Centre for Disaster Protection (the Centre) has launched Labs, a new innovation platform designed to accelerate practical solutions that help governments better plan, finance, and respond to disasters. This means prioritising solutions for the scale and complexity of modern disasters; working collaboratively and sharing knowledge openly; and developing tools rooted in real policy and operational needs to accelerate effective and scalable pre-arranged disaster risk financing. Initial areas of exploration include tools to help governments quantify financial exposure to disasters, design effective parametric triggers for disaster financing, and compare options for structuring disaster risk financing instruments.

item.title: Allianz’s PIMCO, L&G Sued in Scrap Over €1.2 Billion Brussels Tower
item.description: PIMCO and Legal & General Group Plc have been accused of placing “undue pressure” on property valuers in a bitter scrap over the future of a famous Brussels skyscraper whose owner has collapsed into insolvency. The lawyers allege the lenders placed “undue pressure” on valuers at Knight Frank LLP “to reduce its valuation to a figure below €950 million ($1.1 billion).” Large office buildings like the Finance Tower don’t change hands often, so there can be disagreement over true value. The owner of The Finance Tower, the 142 meter (466 feet) high building located in downtown Brussels, filed a lawsuit in the English High Court in a bid to to stall an attempt by its lenders to freeze rental income, according to documents lodged in London and New York courts.

item.title: Oil Tankers Transiting Strait of Hormuz Since Start of Iran War
item.description: Here’s a look at some of the non-Iranian oil tankers that have passed through the strait since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran on February 28, listed by cargo destination, according to data from LSEG and Kpler. has maintained a blockade of Iranian ports, while Iran lifted and then reimposed its own blockade on marine traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz, which typically handles roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil supply. Agios Fanourios I, a VLCC tanker loaded with Iraqi crude and bound for Vietnam crossed the Strait of Hormuz through Iran’s designated route on May 10, Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news agency said.

item.title: Walmart to Pay $230K in Illinois Disability Hiring Discrimination Settlement
item.description: Walmart agreed to pay $230,000 and furnish other relief to settle a disability hiring discrimination lawsuit filed by the U.S. According to the EEOC’s suit, a deaf applicant applied online for a stocking position at a Walmart in Decatur, Illinois. When an associate responsible for screening applicants from Walmart reached out to him to set up an interview, the deaf applicant requested an American Sign Language (ASL) interpreter.

item.title: Sixfold launches underwriting AI solution on Microsoft Marketplace
item.description: Sixfold has announced that its underwriting AI platform is now available through Microsoft Marketplace, allowing insurers to access and deploy the company’s AI capabilities directly within their existing Microsoft Azure environments. According to Sixfold, the Microsoft Marketplace integration is intended to support broader adoption of AI within underwriting operations by simplifying access to the technology inside insurers’ existing cloud ecosystems. By making the platform available through Microsoft Marketplace, carriers can align deployment with existing Azure commitments, cloud budgets and enterprise procurement processes.

item.title: Exzeo launches WindForm Pro to support Florida wind mitigation compliance
item.description: Exzeo has introduced WindForm Pro, a new AI-driven solution designed to help Florida insurers comply with updated wind mitigation inspection requirements issued by the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation (OIR). The company said the platform is designed to reduce administrative workload, improve data consistency and support faster compliance reporting by digitizing and standardizing wind mitigation workflows. The product became available to Florida carriers on May 5 and is intended to automate the processing of wind mitigation inspection forms, which insurers use to assess property resilience and determine eligibility for premium discounts.

item.title: ManageMy integrates with Snowflake to expand insurer data and reporting capabilities
item.description: The post ManageMy integrates with Snowflake to expand insurer data and reporting capabilities appeared first on Insurtech Insights . ManageMy has announced a new integration with Snowflake designed to provide insurance carriers with direct access to operational data and reporting across policy, billing, claims and distribution workflows. The integration connects ManageMy’s insurance platform with Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud infrastructure, enabling carriers to access near real-time reporting, self-service analytics and broader enterprise data connectivity without relying heavily on IT or vendor support.

item.title: Zurich appoints Northern regional market head
item.description: Zurich UK appointed Andy King as Head of Regional Market for the North as the insurer continues to invest in regional growth, broker relationships, and service capabilities. King, who has spent nearly 16 years at Zurich in roles spanning claims, operations, and broker engagement, will lead the Northern region with a broker focused engagement strategy tailored to individual partner needs. The insurer said its Mid Market business has seen strong new business growth this year and plans to hire 15 additional underwriters in the coming months to support demand and maintain service standards.
